tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: RMS Titanic was one of the largest passenger ships of its time and became famous for its tragic sinking in 1912.

tab2.title: History
tab2.content: Titanic struck an iceberg during its maiden voyage from Southampton to New York and sank in the North Atlantic Ocean.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: The Titanic disaster led to major improvements in maritime safety regulations.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: Queen Mary 2 is one of the most famous modern ocean liners used for transatlantic voyages.

tab2.title: Specifications
tab2.content: The ship can carry thousands of passengers and includes luxury amenities such as theaters and restaurants.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: Queen Mary 2 is the flagship of the Cunard Line fleet.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: USS Enterprise is one of the most famous aircraft carriers in the history of the United States Navy.

tab2.title: Specifications
tab2.content: Aircraft carriers serve as floating airbases capable of launching and recovering aircraft.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: The name USS Enterprise has been used for several US Navy ships.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: Ever Given is a massive container ship that became globally famous after blocking the Suez Canal in 2021.

tab2.title: Specifications
tab2.content: The ship can carry over 20,000 shipping containers.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: The blockage caused major disruptions to global shipping and trade.

tab1.title: Description
tab1.content: HMS Victory is a historic warship best known as the flagship of Admiral Nelson during the Battle of Trafalgar.

tab2.title: History
tab2.content: The ship played a major role in British naval history and remains preserved as a museum ship.

tab3.title: Facts
tab3.content: HMS Victory is the oldest commissioned warship in the world.
